short_question_text: Records of mandatory and company defined emergency drills
evidence_item: The company procedures which defined the requirements to conduct onboard emergency response drills, record the outcome and track drills to ensure completion within the defined time frame.
detailed_criteria:
CHECK: Drill register. VERIFY: each mandatory drill performed within frequency required by SOLAS/ISM:
- Fire/abandon-ship: weekly (every 7 days)
- All boats lowered to water: monthly
- Lifeboat engines run free for 3 min: weekly
- Steering gear changeover: 12 hours before each departure
- Enclosed-space rescue: every 2 months
- Search & rescue: per company schedule
NEGATIVE GROUND: gap in drill record >SOLAS interval, or "paper drills" with no real participation evidence.
topic: Records of mandatory and company-defined emergency drills

VERIFY: All boats lowered to water: monthly

VERIFY: Enclosed-space rescue: every 2 months
VERIFY: Search & rescue: per company schedule
VERIFY: NEGATIVE GROUND: gap in drill record >SOLAS interval, or "paper drills" with no real participation evidence

short_question_text: Emergency plans & drills for principal fire scenarios
evidence_item: The shipboard emergency response plans for the principal fire scenarios as applicable to the vessel type.
detailed_criteria:
OBSERVE: Crew respond to a simulated cargo-deck fire or accommodation fire. SUCCESS CRITERIA: - Muster within 5 min, all hands accounted for
- BA team dressed and equipped within 10 min
- Fire pump started and pressure on the line at first hydrant within 8 min
- Emergency fire pump tested independently of main fire pump
- Bridge in voice contact with all team leaders
- Stop cargo/ballast operations confirmed at CCR
- Crew know hose-rolling and basic nozzle handling

short_question_text: Pollution prevention drills required by SOPEP or SMPEP
roviq_sequence: Interview - Deck Officer, Documentation, Bridge, Cargo Control Room
evidence_item: The vessel’s SOPEP or SMPEP.
detailed_criteria:
OBSERVE: Cargo or bunker spill scenario. SUCCESS CRITERIA: - Master notified within 2 min, contact SOPEP Coordinator and Designated Person Ashore (DPA) within 15 min
- Spill kit deployed: oil booms, sorbent pads, drum, shovels, sand
- Source isolated (stop pumping, close valve)
- Crew in PPE before approaching spill
- Drainage scuppers plugged before any cleanup water used
- Spill report drafted while events fresh

short_question_text: Enclosed space rescue emergency response drill.
roviq_sequence: Interview - Deck Rating, Documentation, Cargo Control Room, Main Deck
evidence_item: The shipboard emergency response plan for enclosed space rescue.
detailed_criteria:
OBSERVE: Simulated rescue from confined space (cofferdam, hold space). SUCCESS CRITERIA: - Permit-to-work-in-enclosed-space available for the work being simulated
- Atmosphere test performed before entry — log shows O2 ≥20.5%, LFL=0, toxics within TLV
- Standby person at entrance with comms
- Rescue team has BA + retrieval line
- "Casualty" extracted within 15 min of incident declared
- Stretcher capable of vertical lift used
- First aid administered while waiting for shore medical
topic: Enclosed space rescue emergency response drill

short_question_text: Launching the lifeboat(s) and abandon ship drills
roviq_sequence: Lifeboat deck, Documentation, Bridge, Interview - Deck Rating
evidence_item: The shipboard emergency procedure for abandoning ship.
detailed_criteria:
OBSERVE: Monthly all-boats-to-water OR weekly partial. SUCCESS CRITERIA: - Muster within 3 min of general alarm
- Crew in lifejackets and immersion suits (where required)
- Lifeboat brake released, falls inspected before lowering
- Boat reaches embarkation deck within 5 min
- Boat engine starts within 2 min of being in water
- Boat propels free of ship
- For free-fall boats: drill includes simulated launch using davits (real free-fall on schedule per SOLAS)

subchecks.text: OBSERVE: Monthly all-boats-to-water OR weekly partial

CRITERIA: Crew in lifejackets and immersion suits (where required)

CRITERIA: Boat reaches embarkation deck within 5 min
CRITERIA: Boat engine starts within 2 min of being in water
CRITERIA: Boat propels free of ship

short_question_text: Emergency plan & drills for a cargo vapour or liquid release, Including potential fire

detailed_criteria:
OBSERVE: Simulated LPG vapor cloud release at cargo manifold. SUCCESS CRITERIA: - ESD activated within 30 seconds of detection

- Crew downwind of release evacuate to muster
- Bridge notifies terminal/STS partner and surrounding traffic
- Gas detector readings recorded as drill progresses
- BA team stages upwind of release
- Mayday/Urgency call drafted if appropriate

short_question_text: Emergency plan & drills for failure of electrical power
roviq_sequence: Interview - Deck Officer, Interview - Engineer Officer, Documentation, Bridge, Engine Control Room
evidence_item: The shipboard emergency response plan for the failure of electrical power including any supplementary engineering procedures referenced by the plan.
detailed_criteria:
OBSERVE: Simulated total blackout. SUCCESS: - Emergency gen on within 45 seconds (per SOLAS for tankers)
- Emergency lights illuminate
- Steering gear has power (one of two pumps on EmDb)
- Critical comms maintained (VHF, GMDSS)
- ER troubleshoots cause and restores main bus
- Cargo operations stopped

evidence_item: The company procedures for the maintenance, setting and testing of the cargo tank high-level and overfill alarm systems.
detailed_criteria: TEST: Each tank overfill alarm to be activated either by float tilt test (preferred) or by certified test signal injection. VERIFY: (a) Activation point at 98% volume (per IGC Code 13.3.1 — independent of cargo level gauging). (b) Audible alarm in CCR AND repeating at manifold AND bridge. (c) Alarm light at each above station. (d) Activation must close ESD valves and trip cargo pumps. RECORD: For EACH tank — date of test, found-condition, action taken if defective, reset confirmed. GROUNDS FOR REJECTION: alarm not testable, activation point drifted >2%, no automatic shutdown action, alarm not silenced after correction.

RECORD: For EACH tank — date of test, found-condition, action taken if defective, reset confirmed
RECORD: GROUNDS FOR REJECTION: alarm not testable, activation point drifted >2%, no automatic shutdown action, alarm not silenced after correction

roviq_sequence: Bridge, Cargo Control Room, Main Deck, Cargo Manifold, Interview - Deck Rating
evidence_item: The company procedures for the operation, inspection, maintenance and testing of the vessel’s ESD systems.
detailed_criteria: TEST: ESD activation from EACH independent station — manifold push-buttons (P&S), CCR ESD panel, deck pneumatic stations, shore-link pin pull. VERIFY: (a) Within 10s of activation: all cargo pumps trip, cargo compressors stop. (b) Within 30s: ESD valves at manifold and on tank domes close. (c) Deck water spray system activates if interlocked. (d) Visual + audible alarm at CCR and bridge. CHECK: Spring-return on pneumatic ESD pushbuttons, no jumper wires, no taped-down switches. GROUNDS: Any station fails to activate full sequence, valves close >30s, jumpers found in panel, no system test record in last 3 months.

subchecks.text: TEST: ESD activation from EACH independent station — manifold push-buttons (P&S), CCR ESD panel, deck pneumatic stations, shore-link pin pull
VERIFY: Within 10s of activation: all cargo pumps trip, cargo compressors stop
VERIFY: Within 30s: ESD valves at manifold and on tank domes close
VERIFY: Deck water spray system activates if interlocked
VERIFY: Visual + audible alarm at CCR and bridge
CHECK: Spring-return on pneumatic ESD pushbuttons, no jumper wires, no taped-down switches
CHECK: GROUNDS: Any station fails to activate full sequence, valves close >30s, jumpers found in panel, no system test record in last 3 months

roviq_sequence: Bridge, Cargo Control Room, Main Deck, Cargo Manifold, Interview - Deck Rating

evidence_item: The company procedures for the inspection, maintenance, testing and setting of the cargo tank relief valves.
detailed_criteria: CHECK: Each PRV — set pressure stamp matches Tank Type C MARVS (typically 1.4 bar g for fully-pressurised LPG carriers — verify against Cargo System Operation Manual). VERIFY: (a) Last popping test certificate within 12 months, executed shoreside by approved workshop. (b) Test report shows opening at ±3% set pressure, full lift at +10%. (c) Relief valve isolation valve (if fitted) LOCKED OPEN with positive indication. (d) Vent mast outlet area clear of obstruction, no birds nesting in stack, vent screens intact. GROUNDS: Test certificate >12m old, set pressure differs from MARVS, isolation valve unlocked or status uncertain, mast obstruction.

short_question_text: Maintaining the atmosphere in the interbarrier spaces and/or hold spaces
evidence_item: The company procedures for monitoring the integrity of the containment system and maintaining the atmosphere in the interbarrier spaces and/or hold spaces in a safe condition.
detailed_criteria: For Type C tanks (typical AT/AT10): hold space pressure and oxygen monitoring. CHECK: (a) O2 reading in each hold space — log shows continuous record, value typically inert (<2% O2 if N2 padded). (b) Pressure reading in each hold — slight positive vs atmospheric. (c) Last calibration of sensors <6 months. (d) Alarm setpoints: O2 high alarm at value per COM, pressure low alarm at threshold per COM. GROUNDS: Sensor stuck, calibration expired, alarm setpoints altered without MoC.

evidence_item: The company procedures for the management and operation of the cargo alarm systems.
detailed_criteria: REVIEW: Alarm log for last 30 days — count number of times each critical alarm activated. CHECK FOR: (a) Repeating "nuisance" alarms — these suggest sensor drift or process instability. (b) Inhibited or "shelved" alarms — must have written MoC approval and time-bound restoration plan. (c) Alarm acknowledgement records — who, when. NEGATIVE OBSERVATION GROUND: any alarm permanently silenced/disabled without MoC documentation.

evidence_item: Company procedures for managing cargo and vapour connections at the cargo manifolds.
detailed_criteria: INSPECT: Manifold area, both port and stbd. CHECK: (a) Drip trays under each connection — drain plugged (do not drain hydrocarbon overboard). (b) Manifold valve hand-wheels — turn freely (lift each handwheel + lower). (c) Insulation on manifold spool — intact, dry, not oil-soaked. (d) Blank flanges fitted on unused connections — full set of bolts (no spaces), gasket appropriate for LPG service. (e) SWL plate on manifold support visible, current. (f) Safety shower & eyewash within 25m, tested in last week (date stenciled).

short_question_text: Cargo and vapour pipeline insulation and expansion arrangements
evidence_item: The company procedures for the regular inspection and maintenance of cargo and vapour pipeline insulation and expansion arrangements.
detailed_criteria: INSPECT: walk the cargo pipework end-to-end. CHECK: (a) Insulation intact — no missing sections, no compressed/wet areas. (b) Vapor barrier (cladding) sealed at joints. (c) Expansion loops or bellows free to move — no hard contact with supports, no paint cracking at flexure points. (d) Pipe supports — sliding feet operating, no seized supports. (e) Earth/bonding straps fitted at each flange and across each loop. GROUNDS: water ingress visible in insulation (orange staining), bonding strap missing, support seized.
topic: Cargo and vapour pipeline insulation and expansion arrangements

short_question_text: IGC safety, rescue and recovery equipment
evidence_item: Company procedures for the use of the safety equipment, including SCBAs, required by the IGC Code.
detailed_criteria:
INVENTORY: Per IGC Code 14.4.4: - 1 set of BA + spare cylinder per crew expected to use it (typically 2 sets minimum)
- 1 stretcher capable of being lifted vertically out of tank/hold spaces
- 1 set of medical equipment for cargo type carried (e.g., chemical-burn first aid for NH3 cargoes)
- 1 protective clothing set per officer who may enter cargo area
- 1 safety lamp / explosion-proof torch
- Resuscitator
CHECK: Each item — last inspection date stenciled, condition serviceable, located in designated cargo emergency locker (not the engine room SCBA locker).

short_question_text: Water or liquid cargo leakage into a hold or insulation space
evidence_item: Company procedures for detecting water leakage into a hold or insulation space and for dealing with any water or liquid cargo that may have accumulated in these spaces.
detailed_criteria: CHECK: (a) Hold space drain valves — exercise once per voyage, verify outlet is to a safe location (typically a closed slop tank, not overboard). (b) Drain log shows recent operation. (c) Insulation space — typically dry; if water found in drain test, this is a CRITICAL OBSERVATION and requires investigation per COM.
topic: Liquid or water leakage into hold or insulation space

evidence_item: Company procedures for the operation of the submerged motor electric cargo pumps and the testing of their associated safety devices and alarms.

(c) Bearing temperature alarms set and tested. (d) Cargo pump room ventilation — running before any maintenance entry. (e) Pump trip on overload, on low cargo level (cavitation prevention).

short_question_text: Inerting, gas freeing and gassing up operations
evidence_item: The company procedures that give guidance on cargo tank environmental control during inerting, gas freeing and gassing up operations, thermal load hazards during tank cool-down, and the minimum cargo temperature.
detailed_criteria: CHECK: Procedures in COM cover: (a) Inerting before cargo (purge with N2 until O2 <8%). (b) Gassing up before loading (introduce cargo vapor while displacing N2 — log O2 and HC%). (c) Gas freeing after discharge (purge with N2 until HC <1% LFL, then aerate with air until O2 >20.5%). VERIFY: Last operation log shows correct sequence and held the limits.
topic: Inerting, gas freeing and gassing up operations

subchecks.text: CHECK: Procedures in COM cover:
CHECK: Inerting before cargo (purge with N2 until O2 <8%)
CHECK: Gassing up before loading (introduce cargo vapor while displacing N2 — log O2 and HC%)
CHECK: Gas freeing after discharge (purge with N2 until HC <1% LFL, then aerate with air until O2 >20.5%)
VERIFY: Last operation log shows correct sequence and held the limits

short_question_text: Inerting, gas freeing and gassing up operations
evidence_item: Records of inerting, gassing up and cooling down operations including: — Parameters monitored. — Evidence that gas concentration monitoring was carried out at different tank levels during inerting or gassing up operations. — Actions taken to maintain the interbarrier / hold space pressure. — Nitrogen consumption and, where fitted, running hours of the nitrogen generator.
detailed_criteria: CHECK: Procedures in COM cover: (a) Inerting before cargo (purge with N2 until O2 <8%). (b) Gassing up before loading (introduce cargo vapor while displacing N2 — log O2 and HC%). (c) Gas freeing after discharge (purge with N2 until HC <1% LFL, then aerate with air until O2 >20.5%). VERIFY: Last operation log shows correct sequence and held the limits.

short_question_text: Testing main propulsion, steering gear, thrusters & power generation plant
evidence_item: The company procedures which defined the pre-arrival, pre-departure and/or pre-transit machinery testing requirements.
detailed_criteria:
CHECK: Daily pre-departure ER tests. RECORD: in ER log. ITEMS:
- ME ahead/astern from bridge
- Steering gear pumps individually + both together
- Both rudder extremes tested, rudder-angle indicator agrees
- DG synchronisation
- Bowthruster tested (if fitted) within 12 hr of departure
- Whistle
- Nav lights — main + emergency
- Telegraph and clock check
Sign-off by Chief Eng and Master in deck log.
topic: Testing main propulsion, steering gear, thrusters & power generation plant

evidence_item: The company procedures for the operation and testing of the emergency generator.
detailed_criteria: TEST: (a) Automatic blackout start within 45 seconds of main bus failure. (b) Load test — apply ≥25% nameplate load for at least 30 min, log temperatures. (c) Cooling air vents free, room temperature normal. (d) Fuel day tank — full, sample clear, no water layer. (e) Battery start (separate from main start) — both batteries charged, terminals clean. (f) Start in MANUAL mode confirms muster panel control. PERIODIC: full run-up monthly with load. NEGATIVE GROUND: failure to start within 45s in any test, or run not held for full period.

subchecks.text: TEST: Automatic blackout start within 45 seconds of main bus failure

TEST: Fuel day tank — full, sample clear, no water layer
TEST: Battery start (separate from main start) — both batteries charged, terminals clean
TEST: Start in MANUAL mode confirms muster panel control.

evidence_item: Company procedures for the operation, calibration and maintenance of the EGCS.
detailed_criteria: CHECK: (a) Operation continuous in ECA-S waters. (b) SOx outlet ratio meets MARPOL Annex VI for the alternative fuel — typically SO2/CO2 ≤4.3 (corresponds to 0.10% S equivalent). short_question_text: Remote controls for fuel and lube oil system valves
roviq_sequence: Engine Room, Engine Control Room, Emergency Headquarters.
evidence_item: The company procedures for the inspection, testing and maintenance of the remote controls for fuel and lube oil valves and emergency fuel and lube oil pump shut-offs and oil tank quick closing valves.

TEST: Each test recorded

short_question_text: Fire prevention in machinery spaces - hot surfaces and oil spray
evidence_item: The company procedures that set out the actions to be taken to ensure the integrity of measures in place to prevent fires in the machinery spaces caused by a flammable liquid spraying onto a hot surface.
detailed_criteria: INSPECT: (a) Lagging on hot surfaces (exhaust manifold, turbo casing, steam pipes) — clean and dry, no oil staining. (b) Any oil leaks above hot surfaces — fuel pump rack, injectors, fuel return lines — repair immediately. (c) Drip trays under fuel injection equipment, drained regularly. (d) Fuel oil and lube oil insulation jacketing intact, no exposed metal.
topic: Fire prevention in machinery spaces — hot surfaces and oil spray

subchecks.text: INSPECT: Lagging on hot surfaces (exhaust manifold, turbo casing, steam pipes) — clean and dry, no oil staining

INSPECT: Fuel oil and lube oil insulation jacketing intact, no exposed metal
